[TITLE]: South Carolina Hunter Tags a Massive Alligator Pushing 12 Feet and 700 Pounds
[METADATA/INTRO]: South Carolina Hunter Tags a Massive Alligator Pushing 12 Feet and 700 Pounds Around 8 p.m. on the opening night of South Carolina’s alligator hunting season, Kim Bolen Lawhon helped launch a 17-foot johnboat from Blount’s Landing near Eutawville. Joined...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: South Carolina Hunter Tags a Massive Alligator Pushing 12 Feet and 700 Pounds 
 Around 8 p.m. on the opening night of South Carolina’s alligator hunting season, Kim Bolen Lawhon helped launch a 17-foot johnboat from Blount’s Landing near Eutawville. Joined by her husband and two friends, they searched around the Sixteen Island region of Lake Marion, looking for a gator that measured seven feet or longer. 
 After an hour of cruising and searching with spotlights, they finally glimpsed a likely target. They headed straight for it but the gator quickly went under. One of the hunters cast a line anyway and hooked the gator with a heavy fishing rod.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2013
[TITLE]: Trophy Alligator: Two Lakeland Hunters Bag 12 1/2-Foot Reptile
[METADATA/INTRO]: Trophy Alligator: Two Lakeland Hunters Bag 12 1/2-Foot Reptile LAKELAND | Harris Woodsby and Matt Fellows of Lakeland have killed a half-dozen alligators longer than 10 feet in the past three years....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Having drawn permits for hunting a section of the Kissimmee River below the locks at State Road 60, Woodsby and Fellows dispatched a huge gator that dwarfed any they'd bagged before. 
 The gator measured 12 feet, 6 inches long and weighed 565 pounds. 
 This was the trophy gator they'd been hunting for, the reason they hunt all night long for the adrenaline rush that comes from battling a monstrous prehistoric reptile. 
 "It gets a little bit nervous, especially when you've only got two or three people dealing with an animal that big," said Fellows, 38. "You can make one bad turn and it can be a bad day."

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2017
[TITLE]: BDB: Everybody look east and blow. Irma is a hurricane now
[METADATA/INTRO]: BDB: Everybody look to the east and BLOWWWWWWWW. Irma is now a hurricane. And another giant alligator. Oh Florida. This should do the trick...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Keep your eye on it, and be sure to follow along with our coverage on FLORIDATODAY.com. 
 Another Florida perk: Alligators 
 Come everyone, gather ‘round the campfire. Someone hand me a flashlight, because I’ve got a spooky story to haunt your dreams forevermore. 
 It’s a tale two men and one massive alligator. 
 A hunting duo from Stuart landed the biggest alligator kill so far this year in the Kissimmee River. The beast weighed in at around 750 pounds and was 13 feet long. 
 The hunters were out in a boat when they snagged something along the bottom of the river. It was the alligator. The twist here is that the alligator saw the hook coming and chomped down on it with its giant bite.
